name: Reverse Pivot
preceding_figure_names: Closed Impetus, Natural Spin Turn, Outside Spin, Reverse Turn
following_figure_names: Closed Change LF (when ending DW), Whisk (when ending DW), Double Reverse Spin (when ending DW; or when ending LOD; or when ending DC), Contra Check (when ending DW), Reverse Turn (1-3 into Basic Weave when ending LOD; or when ending DC), Progressive Chasse To R (3/8 turn, when ending LOD; or when ending DC), Closed Telemark (when ending DC), Open Telemark (when ending DC), Fallaway Reverse And Slip Pivot (when ending DC)
notes: If the Reverse Pivot is started backing DC the alignment for the Pivot (Man) would be "to center, toe turned in". Note also that this step is in CBMP owing to the body turning strongly to L as the RF foot slips back. This step is on the half beat, at end of last step of previous figure (e.g. Closed Impetus) - body has not completely lowered in previous figure when stepping on this step. Allow this step to blend smoothly with the preceding step, if necessary borrowing from the next beat.

comments: RF should track under the body.
Body begins turning at the beginning of this step.
Keep head weight on your left.
Watch Lady's position, don't pivot too much too early, which will peel off from lady.
LF fwd in CBMP (small step) RF held in CBMP | DC | TH | up to 1/2 L (Pivot) |
Every time when turning to the left, imagine the belly button and center slightly turning to the right.
Do not allow shoulder to completely line up with hips.
R shoulder and R hip feel slightly open in left turning figures.
Step forward, head goes more left. Show off extension.
Controlled lowering at the end, before next step.
